channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: FG seals AI crop monitoring deal with Morocco, targets 15 states
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigeria’s FG partners with Morocco to deploy an AI crop monitoring system, targeting 15 states to boost food security and agricultural productivity.
Read More: https://punchng.com/fg-seals-ai-crop-monitoring-deal-with-morocco-targets-15-states/

item.title: Burns builds Open lead after Fox's historic round and DeChambeau's fightback
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Sam Burns takes a two-shot lead into the final round of The 154th Open, as Ryan Fox matched major history and Bryson DeChambeau stayed in title contention.

item.title: HUGE BRAWL! Benn and Garcia separated in HEATED press conference!
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Conor Benn and Ryan Garcia exchanged heated words and then had to be separated by security during their Zuffa press conference and face-off.

item.title: Nigeria, Morocco sign satellite-AI monitoring crop deal; rollout begins in 15 states
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nigeria has signed a Memorandum of Understanding to roll out a satellite- and artificial intelligence-driven agricultural monitoring system that will supply federal, state and local governments with near real-time data on farmland, crop distribution, yields and potential food security threats. The p

item.title: Chelsea Agree Record £117m Deal For Villa’s Rogers
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Chelsea have agreed a club record £117m deal to sign England midfielder Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa. The 23-year-old will sign a six-year contract at Stamford Bridge, with an option for a further year. Rogers will have a medical on Monday after returning from international duty with England at th

item.dc:creator: TVC News

This was disclosed in a statement on Saturday by the A

item.title: Hackers take over Kenya’s president’s website, demand 5 Bitcoins ransom
item.dc:creator: Techpoint Africa
item.description: Kenya's president hacked website was defaced with demeaning messages targeting the president; the unknown group responsible for the attack are demanding a ransom of 5 Bitcoins (approximately $317,215).

item.title: Speaker Tajudeen distributes 60,000 bags of fertiliser to constituents
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Speaker of the House of Representatives, Hon. Abbas Tajudeen, distributed 60,000 bags of fertiliser among various segments of his The post Speaker Tajudeen distributes 60,000 bags of fertiliser to constituents appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Radda Courts Katsina Citizens for 2027 Budget Planning
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Francis Sardauna in Katsina Governor Dikko Umaru Radda has courted Katsina State citizens in a town-hall meeting across the 361 wards of the state to collate their inputs and community

item.title: Uzodimma describes Tinubu as Nigeria’s Lee Kuan Yew
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Governor Hope Uzodimma of Imo State has likened President Bola Tinubu to Singapore’s founding leader, Lee Kuan Yew, praising his economic reforms and expressing confidence that Nigerians would re-elect him in 2027. Lee is globally recognized as the founding father of modern Singapore, spearheading i

item.title: Woman allegedly kills husband’s mistress’ two-month-old baby in Benue
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: A 25-year-old woman, identified as Erdoo Ugede, has been arrested for allegedly killing the two-month-old son of her husband's alleged mistress in Ankyoov, Ikyogbaji Council Ward of Gwer East Local Government Area, LGA, of Benue State. The post Woman allegedly kills husband’s mistress’ t

item.title: UNICEF, EU Launch Leadership Capacity Initiative to Strengthen Adamawa Health System
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Funmi Ogundare The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF), in collaboration with Development Governance International (DGI) Consult, has launched a leadership, governance and management capacity-building initiative in Adamawa State aimed at

item.title: ‘Our incoming president’, Isaac Fayose hails Peter Obi
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Businessman Isaac Fayose has described the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi, as Nigeria's 'incoming president' following The post ‘Our incoming president’, Isaac Fayose hails Peter Obi appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: IPAC vows to deliver 2.5m votes for C’River in 2027 polls
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Inter-Party Advisory Council (IPAC), Cross River State chapter, has vowed to mobilize no fewer than 2.5 million votes for the state in the 2027 general elections, citing ongoing voter registration efforts across the 18 local government areas. The post IPAC vows to deliver 2.5m votes for C’

item.title: FG Targets Dec To Commission Kaduna–Kano, Kano–Daura–Katsina Rail Lines
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: …As project hits 75% completion The Federal Government has siad the Kaduna–Kano and Kano–Daura–Katsina sections of the Kano–Maradi Railway Project are on course for commissioning in December 2026. The Permanent Secretary, Federal Ministry of Transportation, Engr. Funsho Adebiyi, gave this assu

item.title: How Chelsea hijacked Arsenal’s key target Morgan Rogers
item.dc:creator: NotJustOk
item.description: Chelsea have reportedly beat Arsenal’s transfer bid for Aston Villa’s Morgan Rodgers with their latest fee. The post How Chelsea hijacked Arsenal’s key target Morgan Rogers appeared first on NotjustOk.

item.title: GOC Urges Commanders To Leave Lasting Legacies Via Welfare Projects
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The GOC encouraged commanders across the Division to continue executing impactful projects, irrespective of their size. The post GOC Urges Commanders To Leave Lasting Legacies Via Welfare Projects appeared first on Channels Television.
